---
title: "Untitled"
author: '2'
date: "2026-05-17"
output: html_document
---
```{r}
data <- cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered

Q15 <- data$Q15

# pares de variables
pairs <- list(
  c("Q17","Q18"),
  c("Q19","Q20"),
  c("Q21","Q22"),
  c("Q31","Q32"),
  c("Q33","Q34"),
  c("Q35","Q36"),
  c("Q37","Q38"),
  c("Q40","Q41")
)

results <- data.frame(
  par = character(),
  cor = numeric()
)

for (p in pairs) {
  x <- data[[p[1]]] / Q15
  y <- data[[p[2]]] / Q15
  
  results <- rbind(results, data.frame(
    par = paste(p[1], p[2], sep = "-"),
    cor = cor(x, y, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")
  ))
}

results
```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)   # para combinar gráficos

# Lista de variables
vars <- c("Q17","Q18","Q19","Q20","Q21","Q22",
          "Q31","Q32","Q33","Q34","Q35","Q36",
          "Q37","Q38","Q40","Q41")

qq_list <- list()
hist_list <- list()

for (v in vars) {
  
  new_name <- paste0(v, "_div_Q15")
  
  vec <- cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered[[v]] /
         cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered$Q15
  
  # QQ-plot
  qq_list[[new_name]] <- ggplot(data.frame(x = vec), aes(sample = x)) +
    stat_qq(color = "steelblue") +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red") +
    ggtitle(paste("QQ-plot:", new_name)) +
    theme_minimal()
  
  # Histograma
  hist_list[[new_name]] <- ggplot(data.frame(x = vec), aes(x)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), bins = 20,
                   fill = "skyblue", color = "white") +
    geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 1) +
    ggtitle(paste("Histograma:", new_name)) +
    theme_minimal()
}

# Combinar todos los QQ-plots en una sola imagen
qq_grid <- wrap_plots(qq_list, ncol = 4)

# Combinar todos los histogramas en una sola imagen
hist_grid <- wrap_plots(hist_list, ncol = 4)

# Mostrar ambos
qq_grid
hist_grid

```
```{r}
library(ggplot2)
library(patchwork)

vars <- c("Q17","Q18","Q19","Q20","Q21","Q22",
          "Q31","Q32","Q33","Q34","Q35","Q36",
          "Q37","Q38","Q40","Q41")

qq_list <- list()
hist_list <- list()

for (v in vars) {
  
  new_name <- paste0(v, "_div_Q15")
  
  vec <- cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered[[v]] /
         cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered$Q15
  
  # QQ-plot con texto y líneas pequeñas
  qq_list[[new_name]] <- ggplot(data.frame(x = vec), aes(sample = x)) +
    stat_qq(color = "steelblue", size = 0.5) +
    stat_qq_line(color = "red", linewidth = 0.3) +
    ggtitle(paste("QQ:", new_name)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8))
  
  # Histograma con densidad y líneas pequeñas
  hist_list[[new_name]] <- ggplot(data.frame(x = vec), aes(x)) +
    geom_histogram(aes(y = ..density..), bins = 20,
                   fill = "skyblue", color = "white", linewidth = 0.2) +
    geom_density(color = "red", linewidth = 0.3) +
    ggtitle(paste("Hist:", new_name)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 8) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 8))
}

# Combinar todos los QQ-plots en una sola imagen
qq_grid <- wrap_plots(qq_list, ncol = 4)

# Combinar todos los histogramas en una sola imagen
hist_grid <- wrap_plots(hist_list, ncol = 4)

qq_grid
hist_grid

```



```{r}
data <- cuidansum_data_netq_anonymized_filtered
Q15 <- data$Q15

pairs <- list(
  c("Q17","Q18"),
  c("Q19","Q20"),
  c("Q21","Q22"),
  c("Q31","Q32"),
  c("Q33","Q34"),
  c("Q35","Q36"),
  c("Q37","Q38"),
  c("Q40","Q41")
)

labels <- c()

cors <- numeric()

for (p in pairs) {
  x <- data[[p[1]]] / Q15
  y <- data[[p[2]]] / Q15
  
  labels <- c(labels, paste0(p[1], "-", p[2]))
  cors <- c(cors, cor(x, y, method="spearman", use="complete.obs"))
}

df <- data.frame(Pair = labels, Correlation = cors)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(x = Pair, y = Correlation)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  ylim(-1,1) +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Correlación Spearman por pares de variables",
       x = "",
       y = "Correlación")

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df, aes(x = Pair, y = 1, fill = Correlation)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(low="red", mid="white", high="blue", midpoint=0) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank()) +
  labs(title="Mapa de correlaciones por pares")
```
```{r}
par(mfrow=c(2,4))

for (p in pairs) {
  
  x <- data[[p[1]]] / Q15
  y <- data[[p[2]]] / Q15
  
  plot(x, y,
       pch=19,
       col=rgb(0,0,1,0.3),
       main=paste(p[1], "-", p[2]),
       xlab=p[1],
       ylab=p[2])
  
  abline(lm(y ~ x), col="red", lwd=2)
}
```
